flow.openTargetDetail: Chromium opens the Website URL for this generated case.

factDetail.title: Detail
docs.title: Passmark TestOps Guide
docs.subtitle: A practical system guide for new users to understand the product purpose, operating flow, run queue, API, setup, and development guardrails.
docs.purposeText: Passmark TestOps helps QC teams generate testcase CSV files with local AI, review or edit them outside the app, import them back, and run safe automation for supported cases.
docs.corePrincipleText: The testcase file is the source of truth. AI creates a QC-ready CSV first; automation is optional and only runs cases that have a safe internal automation mapping.
docs.backendItemServer: Node.js + TypeScript, native HTTP server in
docs.backendItemDb: Prisma is the ORM, PostgreSQL is the primary database.
docs.backendItemAi: The Ollama/OpenAI-compatible AI client lives in
docs.backendItemQueue: Imported automation runs use an in-memory queue, default concurrency is 1.
docs.frontendItemI18n: Dark dashboard UI, VI/EN support through
docs.frontendItemApi: The frontend calls backend APIs and never calls local AI directly.
docs.frontendItemPoll: Run Center polls imported run results through
docs.userStepProject: Choose or create a project and target so the testcase file has the right URL/context.
docs.userStepTarget: Use Create testcase file to describe the product, risk area, or pages that need coverage.
docs.userStepSuite: Click Generate CSV. Ollama estimates coverage and returns a QC-ready testcase file.
docs.userStepCase: Download the CSV, review it like a tester, and edit rows in Excel or Google Sheets if needed.
docs.userStepRun: Switch to Auto test from file, import the reviewed CSV, then click Run imported.
docs.userStepResult: Open View details or Reports to read AI explanation, testcase rows, run results, failures, and exported result CSV.
docs.targetWebPurpose: Test a public or staging website.
docs.targetLocalPurpose: Test a local web app running on the machine.
docs.targetApiPurpose: Store an endpoint/base URL for a later API testing phase.
docs.targetSourcePurpose: Store a source path for a later source scan phase.
docs.targetFallbackPrefix: Targets attach URL/base context to generated testcase files.
docs.targetFallbackSuffix: is used as the fallback when no separate target is selected.
docs.runStepPoll: Frontend shows the CSV file, row count, download/import controls, and the detail panel.
docs.runStepWorkerPrefix: The tester can edit the CSV outside the app, then import it in
docs.runStepStoreSuffix: the backend runs only safely mapped automation cases through Playwright.
docs.runStepDonePrefix: Results are saved to history as
docs.restartPrefix: When the backend starts again, old runs still in
docs.customCaseEstimate: AI estimates the needed testcase count before generating rows.
docs.customCasePlan: AI returns the coverage reason so QC can understand why those cases exist.
docs.customCaseCode: CSV rows include objective, preconditions, data, steps, expected result, priority, severity, and automation candidate.
docs.customCaseFallback: If AI returns malformed JSON, fallback still creates a usable testcase file.
docs.customCaseProgress: Imported automation results can be opened while the run continues.
docs.customCaseRead: Manual or unsupported cases stay in the CSV and are marked as skipped/manual during automation.
docs.seoEnabledText: CSV is the main review artifact. The automation runner only executes cases mapped to safe internal automation kinds; manual cases remain available for tester review.
docs.aiBaseResourceNote: and limits resources with a small model, low context, and 1 loaded model.
docs.ruleApi: Do not change API response format unless truly needed.
docs.ruleFrontendAi: Do not let the frontend call local AI directly.
docs.ruleEnv: Do not hardcode AI URL/key/model in source code; always read them from
docs.ruleSqlite: Do not return to SQLite for the main flow; SQLite is legacy migration only.
docs.ruleNoTs: Do not let AI create dangerous, destructive, stress, or DDoS tests.
docs.ruleRenderer: Keep the template renderer as the stable source of Playwright code.
docs.ruleQueue: The current queue is in-memory and designed for a future Redis/BullMQ upgrade.
docs.troubleDockerTextSuffix: open Docker Desktop first, then run
docs.troublePrismaText: Stop any server/node process that is holding the Prisma engine, then run
docs.troublePlaywrightTextSuffix: if the machine does not have the Chromium runtime yet.
docs.roadmapApi: API testing runner for target type
docs.roadmapUser: Multi-user support, roles, and audit log.
docs.roadmapReport: PDF/DOCX report export and defect trend dashboard.
